rule: Use standard section headings: Experience, Education, Skills, Projects, Certifications. ATS systems look for these exact headers to parse resume structure.
Avoid tables, columns, text boxes, graphics, and headers/footers. Most ATS parsers read left-to-right, top-to-bottom plain text and will mangle multi-column layouts.
Use exact keyword spelling from the job description. ATS systems match strings exactly: 'Node.js' and 'NodeJS' are different tokens. Mirror the JD's phrasing precisely.
Place the most important job keywords in at least 2 sections (e.g., Skills AND Experience bullets). Higher keyword density across sections increases ATS ranking weight.
Workday ATS scores resumes based on keyword overlap with job requisitions. It weights skills section heavily — list all required technologies explicitly in a dedicated Skills section.
Greenhouse uses semantic matching, not just exact strings. It understands synonyms (React.js vs React) but still prefers exact JD phrasing for higher confidence scores.
Lever scores candidates on skills tags extracted from resumes. Ensure technologies appear as standalone recognizable terms, not buried in long sentences.
Submit resumes as .pdf or .docx. PDFs must not be scanned images — they must contain selectable text. Image-based PDFs score 0 in ATS parsing.
Start each experience bullet with a strong action verb. ATS systems and recruiters both parse the first word of each bullet as the primary action signal.
Include both the full name and common abbreviation when both appear in the JD: 'Amazon Web Services (AWS)', 'Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD)'. ATS matches both forms.
Use consistent date format: 'Mon YYYY' (e.g., Jan 2023) or 'MM/YYYY'. ATS date parsers expect standard formats to extract employment duration correctly.
Spell out degree names fully: 'Bachelor of Science in Computer Science' not 'B.Sc CS'. ATS education parsers match against standard degree taxonomies.
iCIMS ranks candidates on completeness of profile sections. Ensure all sections (Summary, Experience, Education, Skills) are present and non-empty.
The Summary/Objective section is parsed first and weighted heavily. Include the job title, primary technology stack, and 1-2 key achievements in the summary.
Do not keyword-stuff. Modern ATS systems (Greenhouse, Lever) use NLP and flag unnaturally high keyword density as spam. Weave keywords into natural sentences.
Include a Projects section with tech stacks. ATS systems extract technologies from tech_stack lists directly. Name each technology as it appears on the JD.
SmartRecruiters uses AI scoring that weighs years of experience with specific technologies. Mention technology names alongside time context in bullets where possible.
List certifications with issuer and year. ATS systems match certifications to job requirements — include exact cert names: 'AWS Certified Solutions Architect', not 'AWS cert'.
